Policy 'qa-staging', version 2026.07.1.
Content hash: 6b0e6620c25aebb67a2e3a3829c8841c77937e867dfeeb6bea87101487ed523a.

Prepared for 'ci-runner', resolved by environment. Groups: QA-Automation.

Mode: observe (shadow). Nothing is blocked by this policy: would-deny events are recorded to the audit log and the calls proceed. Observation is not protection.

Where agents may read and write, in match order (the first matching domain wins):
(a pattern like 'example.com' matches only that exact host; '*.example.com' matches its subdomains and never example.com itself)
  1. Full access on *.staging.geisinger.org: agents may read and act. Purpose: Full automation on staging.
  2. Read-only on geisinger.org, *.geisinger.org: agents may read pages but not act on them. This grant always enforces: its denials block even when the policy mode is observe. Purpose: Read-only verification on production.
  3. Write-only on forms.staging.geisinger.org: agents may act on pages but not read them (write does not include read). Only these tools: form_input. Purpose: Submit test forms only.
Any domain not matched above would be denied; in this mode that denial is recorded, not blocked.

This policy locks no settings and sets no defaults; users keep control of every setting.

On a would-deny the agent sees the ordinary tool result and no denial text. The denial id appears only in the audit record, as decision 'shadow_deny'.

Warnings:
  - grant 'form-writer': access "write" does not include read; agents can act on pages they cannot read. Most policies want "read" or "all".
